rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFFS JOHN GIDDING, PIVOTAL INC., MIDSHORE MARKETING Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint. Motion denied without prejudice as untimely in light of the current trial date. Adding a new cause of action and Doe defendants would necessitate a trial continuance, adding additional expenses and prejudice to defendants. Plaintiffs may file a partial dismissal if they seek to delete certain causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY OF MODESTO VS. AUTHORITY FOR CALIFORNIA CITIES EXCESS LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE STATE OF PENNSYLVANIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Motion fails to comply with CRC Rule 3.1350, subsections (b) and (g). Evidence introduced in support of motion also fails to comply with CRC Rules 3.1110, 3.1113 and 3.1116. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SONIA G MARTINEZ VS. MARGUERITE P PROLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn For Order Appointing Guardian Ad Litem For Deft ; Memo Of P And A; Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF SONIA MARTINEZ Motion For Order Appointing Guardian Ad Litem For Defendant. Motion denied. Insufficient evidence presented to show that defendant cannot understand the nature of the lawsuit or that she cannot communicate effectively with her counsel. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KUSHAM GUPTA et al VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside The Order Sustaining Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 8, PLAINTIFFS KUSHAM GUPTA, SHASHI GUPTA Motion To Vacate and Set Aside the Order Sustaining Demurrer. Motion denied. Defendant's counsel provided notice of intention to appear on April 29, 2009 and contest the court's tentative ruling as described in his November 2nd supplemental declaration. Plaintiffs are responsible for advising court and counsel of current and best telephone number to be reached for court related business. No excusable neglect, mistake, surprise, inadvertence or extrinsic fraud demonstrated. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ALIPHCOM, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. ELIZABETH BASTIAANSE HAMREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 11, CROSS DEFENDANT ALIPHCOM, DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to second cause of action for sexual harassment to allege facts supporting severe or pervasive level. Demurrer overruled as to third cause of action for retaliation. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to fifth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy. Stock options are not wages (International Business Machines Corp. vs. Bajorek (9th Cir. 1999) 191 F.3d 1033, 1039-40.) Motion to strike is denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH WONG VS. SUET YIM DIANA LAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Omtion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action; Alternatively, To Require Pltf To File Pltf; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 3, 2009, line 14, DEFENDANT SUET LAU Motion To Expunge Notice of Pendency of Action, alternatively to require plaintiff to file undertaking.
